ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the employee (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "employee_type" TEXT /* Type of employee (e.g., full-time, part-time, contract) */,
  "work_email" TEXT /* Email address used for work-related communication */,
  "personal_email" TEXT /* Email address used for personal communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department manager */,
  "department_head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  "parent_department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shifts" (
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shift */,
  "shift_name" TEXT /* Name of the shift */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the shift */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the shift */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the shift (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the shift is associated with */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the shift */,
  "shift_type" TEXT /* Type of shift (e.g., morning, evening, night) */,
  "shift_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the shift in hours */,
  "break_time" INTEGER /* Duration of breaks in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shift_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shifts_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each schedule entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee assigned to the shift */,
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shift */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the shift */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the schedule (e.g., confirmed, pending, canceled) */,
  "schedule_type" TEXT /* Type of schedule (e.g., one-time, recurring) */,
  "recurring_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of recurring schedules (e.g., daily, weekly, bi-weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schedule_shift_id FOREIGN KEY ("shift_id") REFERENCES shifts ("shift_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schedule_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attendance" (
  "attendance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attendance record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shift */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the shift */,
  "clock_in_time" TEXT /* Time the employee clocked in */,
  "clock_out_time" TEXT /* Time the employee clocked out */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the attendance (e.g., present, absent, late) */,
  "attendance_status_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the attendance status */,
  "attendance_note" TEXT /* Additional notes about the attendance record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attendance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attendance_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attendance_shift_id FOREIGN KEY ("shift_id") REFERENCES shifts ("shift_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "breaks" (
  "break_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each break */,
  "attendance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attendance record */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the break */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the break */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the break in minutes */,
  "break_type" TEXT /* Type of break (e.g., regular, overtime, meal) */,
  "break_note" TEXT /* Additional notes about the break */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("break_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_breaks_attendance_id FOREIGN KEY ("attendance_id") REFERENCES attendance ("attendance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "leave_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each leave request */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the leave */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the leave */,
  "leave_type" TEXT /* Type of leave (e.g., vacation, sick leave) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., approved, pending, denied) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the leave */,
  "leave_request_status_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the status of the leave request */,
  "leave_request_note" TEXT /* Additional notes about the leave request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_leave_requests_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "overtime" (
  "overtime_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each overtime record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the overtime */,
  "hours_worked" REAL /* Number of hours worked */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the overtime (e.g., approved, pending, denied) */,
  "overtime_type" TEXT /* Type of overtime (e.g., voluntary, mandatory) */,
  "overtime_note" TEXT /* Additional notes about the overtime */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("overtime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_overtime_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., shift change, absence alert) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  "notification_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the notification (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "notification_read_status" TEXT /* Whether the employee has read the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the user accessed the system */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view, modify) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., desktop, mobile, web) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance" (
  "compliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the compliance check */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the compliance (e.g., compliant, non-compliant) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments on the compliance check */,
  "compliance_check_type" TEXT /* Type of compliance check */,
  "compliance_note" TEXT /* Additional notes about the compliance check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compliance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date the policy became effective */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the policy expires */,
  "policy_category" TEXT /* Category of the policy (e.g., labor laws, company policies) */,
  "policy_version" TEXT /* Version of the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)
